(formerly the *Bloor Cinema* and the *Bloor Hot Docs Cinema*) is a movie theatre in [the Annex](/wiki/The_Annex "The Annex") district of [downtown](/wiki/Downtown_Toronto "Downtown Toronto") [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"), Canada, located on the 506 [Bloor Street](/wiki/Bloor_Street "Bloor Street") West, near its intersection with [the Bathurst Street](/wiki/Bathurst_Street_(Toronto) "Bathurst Street (Toronto)") and the [Bathurst subway station](/wiki/Bathurst_station_(Toronto) "Bathurst station (Toronto)").
["Hot Docs Completes Sale of Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema"](https://povmagazine.com/hot-docs-completes-sale-of-hot-docs-ted-rogers-cinema/). *Point of View Magazine*. Retrieved 2025-09-15.

["Hot Docs Receives $5-Million Gift From Rogers Foundation"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160813131506/http://www.hotdocs.ca/news/hot-docs-receives-5-million-gift-from-rogers-foundation). Hot Docs. Archived from [the original](http://www.hotdocs.ca/news/hot-docs-receives-5-million-gift-from-rogers-foundation) on August 13, 2016. Retrieved June 23, 2016.

["'Crucial for us to take this step now': Hot Docs to close its flagship cinema for three months"](https://web.archive.org/web/20250413072904/https://www.cp24.com/news/2024/05/22/crucial-for-us-to-take-this-step-now-hot-docs-to-close-its-flagship-cinema-for-three-months/). *[CP24](/wiki/CP24 "CP24")*. May 22, 2024. Archived from [the original](https://www.cp24.com/news/2024/05/22/crucial-for-us-to-take-this-step-now-hot-docs-to-close-its-flagship-cinema-for-three-months/) on April 13, 2025. Retrieved April 13, 2025.

["Hot Docs Fest Explores Selling Cinema to Ease Financial Crunch"](https://web.archive.org/web/20241020165027/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/hot-docs-fest-explores-selling-cinema-1236034448/). *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")*. October 17, 2024. Archived from [the original](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/hot-docs-fest-explores-selling-cinema-1236034448/) on October 20, 2024. Retrieved October 17, 2024.

["Hot Docs sells its cinema for $6.25 million, but will continue leasing it"](https://web.archive.org/web/20250812014407/https://www.ctvnews.ca/toronto/local/article/hot-docs-sells-its-cinema-for-625-million-but-will-continue-leasing-it/). *[CTV News](/wiki/CTV_News "CTV News")*. August 11, 2025. Archived from [the original](https://www.ctvnews.ca/toronto/local/article/hot-docs-sells-its-cinema-for-625-million-but-will-continue-leasing-it/) on August 12, 2025. Retrieved August 11, 2025.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Bloor Cinema](/w/index.php?title=Bloor_Cinema&redirect=no "Bloor Cinema"))

Movie theatre in Toronto, Ontario

| Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema | |
| --- | --- |

| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [43°39′56″N 79°24′38″W﻿ / ﻿43.665485°N 79.410434°W﻿ / 43.665485; -79.410434](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hot_Docs_Ted_Rogers_Cinema&params=43.665485_N_79.410434_W_region:CA-ON_type:landmark) |

The **Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema** (formerly the *Bloor Cinema* and the *Bloor Hot Docs Cinema*) is a movie theatre in [the Annex](/wiki/The_Annex "The Annex") district of [downtown](/wiki/Downtown_Toronto "Downtown Toronto") [Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto"), [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario"), Canada, located on the 506 [Bloor Street](/wiki/Bloor_Street "Bloor Street") West, near its intersection with [the Bathurst Street](/wiki/Bathurst_Street_(Toronto) "Bathurst Street (Toronto)") and the [Bathurst subway station](/wiki/Bathurst_station_(Toronto) "Bathurst station (Toronto)").

The venue serves as the primary home to the annual [Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival](/wiki/Hot_Docs_Canadian_International_Documentary_Festival "Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival"), as well as screening a regular theatrical lineup of documentary films throughout the year and serving as a venue for other smaller film festivals and cultural events.

In August 2025, it was announced that the theater was acquired by a local buyer.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Docs_Ted_Rogers_Cinema&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In 1913, the Madison Picture Palace (designed by J.A. Mackenzie) opened at this location. It was demolished in 1940 and rebuilt by Kaplan and Sprachman (Harold Solomon Kaplan and Abraham Sprachman)[[2]](#cite_note-2) as the Midtown Theatre. It was renamed the Capri in 1967. In 1973, it became the Eden, showing adult films.

It became the Bloor in 1979 and returned to showing first-run films. One year later, it was sold, becoming an independent repertory-style theatre. It was sold to the Blue Ice Group in 2011 and to the Hot Docs Festival in 2016.[[3]](#cite_note-hist-3)

For a large part of recent history, the Bloor was a [second-run theatre](/wiki/Discount_theater "Discount theater"), showing movies that had already been in theatres, usually before they were released on video and DVD. It screened classic films, [art films](/wiki/Art_film "Art film"), and [cult films](/wiki/Cult_film "Cult film"); *[The Rocky Horror Picture Show](/wiki/The_Rocky_Horror_Picture_Show "The Rocky Horror Picture Show")* was traditionally screened with a live cast on [Halloween](/wiki/Halloween "Halloween") and on the last Friday of every month.

The Bloor Cinema was repeatedly selected as the best repertory cinema in Toronto by *[Eye Weekly](/wiki/Eye_Weekly "Eye Weekly")*. The theatre was independent and reopened after its renovation in 1999.

## Hot Docs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Docs_Ted_Rogers_Cinema&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Hot Docs")]

Although it was closed in 2010, the Bloor Cinema's owner turned away developers looking to replace the theatre. In 2011, it was sold to the Blue Ice Group which managed the cinema in partnership with the Hot Docs, where it was renovated and reopened under the moniker the Bloor Hot Docs Cinema in 2012. It is the main location for the Hot Docs, akin to the [Toronto International Film Festival](/wiki/Toronto_International_Film_Festival "Toronto International Film Festival")'s [Lightbox](/wiki/TIFF_Lightbox "TIFF Lightbox").[[3]](#cite_note-hist-3)

On June 23, 2016, it was announced that the Hot Docs had purchased the Bloor Cinema from the Blue Ice Group, using a CA$4 million gift from the [Rogers Foundation](/wiki/Edward_S._Rogers_Jr.#Philanthropy "Edward S. Rogers Jr."), and that the cinema would be rebranded as the Hot Docs Ted Rogers Cinema.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In the wake of a staffing upheaval that had impacted the 2024 edition of the festival, the Hot Docs announced the temporary closure of the theatre for three months starting June 12 as a cost-cutting measure.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The theatre reopened in September 2024 for third party rentals.

Hot Docs programming at the theatre resumed in December 2024. Hot Docs sought a new owner for the theatre and wanted to secure a lease-back arrangement, which is "part of its longer-term rebuilding strategy", according to a statement by the festival.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2025, the theatre was sold to an anonymous buyer for $6.25 million. Hot Docs continues to run the theatre.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hot_Docs_Ted_Rogers_Cinema&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of cinemas in Toronto](/wiki/List_of_cinemas_in_Toronto "List of cinemas in Toronto")
